Identity.History: Craterus was one of Alexander the Great’s most trusted Macedonian generals, admired for discipline, loyalty, and popularity with the troops. He commanded infantry and cavalry in major campaigns across Asia and often handled difficult assignments, including battles in Persia, Central Asia, and India. Near the end of Alexander’s life, Craterus was ordered to lead veterans back to Macedonia, but Alexander died before the plan was completed. In the Wars of the Successors, Craterus allied with Antipater against Perdiccas, but was killed in battle against Eumenes in 321 BC.
